_comment: Inline policy for the IAM principal running infra/phase3_deploy.py.
This is NOT the Lambda runtime role — this is the human/CI identity that executes the deploy script.
Replace ACCOUNT_ID and REGION with your actual values before use.
Permission sources (by deploy step):
Step 1  — sts:GetCallerIdentity          : resolve account ID at script start
Step 2  — iam:CreateRole                 : create ab-gateway-controller-role
Step 2  — iam:GetRole                    : idempotency check if role exists
Step 2  — iam:PutRolePolicy              : attach inline policy to controller role
Step 3  — lambda:CreateFunction          : deploy traffic controller Lambda
Step 3  — lambda:UpdateFunctionCode      : idempotency — update code if Lambda exists
Step 3  — lambda:UpdateFunctionConfiguration : idempotency — update config if Lambda exists
Step 3  — lambda:GetFunction             : retrieve ARN after update
Step 3  — lambda:GetFunctionConfiguration: used internally by function_active_v2 waiter
Step 4  — sns:CreateTopic                : create ab-gateway-alerts topic (idempotent)
Step 4  — sns:Subscribe                  : add email endpoint to topic
Step 5  — events:PutRule                 : create 15-minute EventBridge schedule rule
Step 5  — events:PutTargets              : set traffic controller Lambda as rule target
Step 5  — lambda:AddPermission           : grant EventBridge permission to invoke Lambda
Step 6  — cloudwatch:PutMetricAlarm      : create 3 alarms (error rate, p95, heartbeat)
Step 7  — cloudwatch:PutDashboard        : create 7-panel ABGateway-Dashboard
Tests   — cloudwatch:GetDashboard        : test_traffic_controller.py Test 6
Tests   — cloudwatch:DescribeAlarms      : test_traffic_controller.py Test 7
Tests   — lambda:InvokeFunction          : test_traffic_controller.py Test 1 + Test 2

Statement._comment: Scoped to the specific controller role only.
Scoped to the traffic controller Lambda function only.
Required for lambda:CreateFunction and lambda:UpdateFunctionConfiguration to accept the role ARN.
Scoped to the single alerts topic.
Scoped to the single controller schedule rule.
Scoped to the 3 named alarms and the single dashboard.
Read permissions needed by test_traffic_controller.py (Test 6 + Test 7).
Needed by test_traffic_controller.py Test 1 + Test 2 to invoke the controller directly.
